sections.paragraph: an unused root, to which, in Syriac, the signification to inquire, is ascribed by Castell (Lex. Syr. h. v.) incorrectly reading and copying the words of Bar Bahlul, whom he follows, from which (see them accurately transcribed in pref. to edit. 3 [Germ.] p. xxi), and hence it appears that B. Bahlul attributes to it the sense of calumniating, i.q. Syr. ܛܪܦܰ. It is more probable, that in Hebrew it had the same power as the Arab. تَرِفَ to live comfortably, whence تُرْفَةُ life abounding in good things and comforts, to which answers the Sanscrit, trip, to delight, Greek, τέρπομαι. Hence-
